task: Write a Python function truncate_words(s, n) returning the first n whitespace-separated words of the string joined by single spaces, so runs of whitespace collapse. When n is zero or negative return ''. When the string has fewer than n words return them all. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function meets the specified requirements, but could be more concise.
trace: The function takes a string `s` and an integer `n` as input. It first splits the string into words using the split() method, which separates the string at each whitespace character. If the number of words in the resulting list is less than `n`, it returns all the words concatenated by single spaces. Otherwise, it returns the first `n` words joined by single spaces. The edge case I've named is when the input string has fewer than `n` words; in this case, the function will still return a valid output containing all the words, rather than raising an exception or returning an error message.
